{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Social Media Handler & Content Creator",
  "description": "At Slomadaz Furnitures we produce premuim home furnitures that combines both comfort and durability. We are a leading name in craftmanship and customer satisfaction. We specialize in bespoke sofa alongside variety of other home furnishings,designed to complement and enhance the unique style of our client.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Handler &amp; Content Creator
Job Location:&nbsp;Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a creative and consistent Social Media Handler &amp; Content Creator to manage our online presence at Slomandaz Furniture.
	Your main role is to create content, post regularly, and help attract customers through social media. You will take videos and pictures of furniture, post them online, and respond to messages from customers.
	This role is for someone who is active, creative, and understands how social media works.


Key Responsibilities


	Create videos and pictures of furniture in the showroom and factory.
	Post content regularly on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
	Write simple captions that help sell products.
	Reply to DMs, comments, and WhatsApp enquiries.
	Come up with content ideas to attract more customers.
	Work with the team to show new designs, production process, and finished work.


Requirements


	Must live in Port Harcourt.
	Must know how to use Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook very well.
	Basic video recording and editing skills (CapCut or similar).
	Creative, consistent, and willing to learn.
	Good communication skills (written and spoken).
	Experience in content creation or social media management is a plus.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and samples of your work (Instagram page, videos, or portfolio) to:&nbsp;info@slomandazglobal.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-02",

  },

Social Media Handler & Content Creator Job at Slomadaz Furnitures




        
    

        
        At Slomadaz Furnitures we produce premuim home furnitures that combines both comfort and durability. We are a leading name in craftmanship and customer satisfaction. We specialize in bespoke sofa alongside variety of other home furnishings,designed to complement and enhance the unique style of our client.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Handler & Content Creator
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a creative and consistent Social Media Handler & Content Creator to manage our online presence at Slomandaz Furniture.
	Your main role is to create content, post regularly, and help attract customers through social media. You will take videos and pictures of furniture, post them online, and respond to messages from customers.
	This role is for someone who is active, creative, and understands how social media works.


Key Responsibilities


	Create videos and pictures of furniture in the showroom and factory.
	Post content regularly on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
	Write simple captions that help sell products.
	Reply to DMs, comments, and WhatsApp enquiries.
	Come up with content ideas to attract more customers.
	Work with the team to show new designs, production process, and finished work.


Requirements


	Must live in Port Harcourt.
	Must know how to use Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook very well.
	Basic video recording and editing skills (CapCut or similar).
	Creative, consistent, and willing to learn.
	Good communication skills (written and spoken).
	Experience in content creation or social media management is a plus.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and samples of your work (Instagram page, videos, or portfolio) to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

N90,000 - N105,000 / Month.

title: Job Openings at Slomadaz Furnitures
content:
At Slomadaz Furnitures we produce premuim home furnitures that combines both comfort and durability. We are a leading name in craftmanship and customer satisfaction. We specialize in bespoke sofa alongside variety of other home furnishings,designed to complement and enhance the unique style of our client.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Storekeeper

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a responsible, organized, and detail-oriented Store Keeper to join our team.
	The Store Keeper will be responsible for managing inventory, ensuring timely allocation of materials to the production team, and maintaining accurate stock records.
	The ideal candidate must be proficient in Microsoft Word and Excel and be willing to learn inventory management software.


Key Responsibilities


	Willingness to learn and operate Erp systems.
	Keep accurate records of all raw materials, tools, and finished products in the store.
	Ensure timely distribution of materialsand other production materials to the manufacturing team
	Keep the storage area clean, well-arranged, and properly labeled
	Maintain detailed records of incoming and outgoing stock, including invoices and delivery notes
	Prevent theft and misappropriation of store items by implementing proper security measures
	Work closely with the production, procurement, and logistics teams to ensure smooth operations.
	Strong Leadership skills- Supervise and manage assistant storekeepers


Minimum Requirements


	2-3 inventory experience in storekeeping or inventory control.
	EXperience in manufacturing or furniture environment is preferred.
	Strong organizational and record-keeping skills.
	Must have proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, etc.).
	Ability to track stock levels, identify shortages, and coordinate reorders.
	Physical fitness to handle materials and inventory when needed.
	Strong attention to details
	Reliable, punctual, and able to work with minimal supervision


Application Closing Date: 22nd April, 2026. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Sales Representative

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are hiring a Sales Representative to join our team at Slomandaz Furniture.
	This role is mainly for the showroom.
	You will attend to walk-in customers, help them choose furniture, and follow up on those who showed interest.
	The right person must be smart, friendly, good at talking to people, and ready to close sales.
	You should also know how to use a computer.


Responsibilities


	Welcome and attend to walk-in customers in the showroom.
	Understand what the customer wants and suggest the right furniture.
	Send quotations and invoices using Microsoft Word or Excel.
	Follow up with leads from WhatsApp, Instagram, or showroom visits.
	Keep the showroom clean and well arranged at all times.
	Build a good relationship with customers so they can return or refer others.
	Record sales and customer details for management updates.


Requirements


	Must live in or around Ada-George, Ikwerre Road, or New GRA for easy commute.
	Sales experience is required (experience in furniture sales is a strong advantage).
	Must know how to use a computer (basic use of Microsoft Word, Excel, or similar).
	Must have good communication skills - polite, confident, and helpful.
	Must be organized, hardworking, and willing to learn fast.
	Should have a positive attitude and be able to work well with others.


Salary
N100,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 2nd May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

3.) Social Media Handler & Content Creator

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a creative and consistent Social Media Handler & Content Creator to manage our online presence at Slomandaz Furniture.
	Your main role is to create content, post regularly, and help attract customers through social media. You will take videos and pictures of furniture, post them online, and respond to messages from customers.
	This role is for someone who is active, creative, and understands how social media works.


Key Responsibilities


	Create videos and pictures of furniture in the showroom and factory.
	Post content regularly on Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
	Write simple captions that help sell products.
	Reply to DMs, comments, and WhatsApp enquiries.
	Come up with content ideas to attract more customers.
	Work with the team to show new designs, production process, and finished work.


Requirements


	Must live in Port Harcourt.
	Must know how to use Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook very well.
	Basic video recording and editing skills (CapCut or similar).
	Creative, consistent, and willing to learn.
	Good communication skills (written and spoken).
	Experience in content creation or social media management is a plus.


Application Closing Date: 2nd May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and samples of your work (Instagram page, videos, or portfolio) to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Financial Analyst

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

About the Role


	As an Analyst&nbsp;in our finance and business consultancy firm, you will play a critical role in ensuring the integrity and accuracy of financial information, compliance with regulations, and the overall quality of our services.


Core Responsibilities
Your responsibilities will include but are not limited to:
Financial Auditing:


	Assist in planning and conducting financial audits for clients to evaluate their financial statements and internal controls.
	Review financial documents, records, and reports to identify discrepancies or irregularities.
	Perform substantive and analytical audit procedures to assess the accuracy of financial information.


Compliance Assessment:


	Assist in assessing and ensuring compliance with relevant financial regulations, standards, and laws.
	Stay up-to-date with changes in financial regulations and communicate their implications to the team.


Data Analysis:


	Analyze financial data and trends to identify areas for improvement or potential risks.
	Prepare reports summarizing findings and recommendations for clients and internal stakeholders.


Documentation:


	Maintain organized and comprehensive files with all relevant documentation.
	Prepare work papers, audit reports, and other documentation as required.
	Prepare and present reports, summaries, and visualizations for internal and client use.


Client Engagement:


	Collaborate with clients and team members to gather necessary information and address their inquiries.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships by providing excellent service and professional guidance.
	Assist clients in understanding complex financial data and reports.


Risk Assessment:


	Assist in assessing the internal control environment of clients to identify weaknesses and suggest improvements.
	Identify and communicate potential risks to the Partners.


Quality Assurance:


	Ensure that audit procedures are conducted in accordance with professional standards and company policies.
	Participate in quality control reviews and process improvement initiatives.
	Training and Development:
	Continuously update your knowledge and skills related to auditing and financial regulations.
	Actively participate in training programs and mentorship opportunities.


Financial Modelling:


	Build and maintain financial models to support decision-making processes for clients.
	Evaluate the financial performance and viability of various projects.


Confidentiality:


	Maintain the highest level of confidentiality and ethical standards in handling sensitive client information.


Reporting:


	Prepare and present audit findings and recommendations to clients and management.
	Assist in the preparation of audit reports and management letters.


Communication:


	Effectively communicate analysis results and insights to clients and internal stakeholders.
	Collaborate with team members to ensure the delivery of high-quality services.


Continuous Learning:


	Stay current with industry trends, data analysis techniques, and compliance best practices.
	Participate in training and professional development activities.


Job Requirements


	ICAN qualified.
	5 years of experience including audit firm experience; preferably big 4.
	Experience in ERP implementation preferably of Odoo software.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to:&nbsp;hrm@givanascosmetics.com&nbsp;using &quot;Financial Analyst&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

Financial Analyst Job at Givanas Cosmetics Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Givanas Cosmetics Nigeria Limited, a manufacturing company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Financial Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

About the Role


	As an Analyst in our finance and business consultancy firm, you will play a critical role in ensuring the integrity and accuracy of financial information, compliance with regulations, and the overall quality of our services.


Core Responsibilities
Your responsibilities will include but are not limited to:
Financial Auditing:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in planning and conducting financial audits for clients to evaluate their financial statements and internal controls.
	Review financial documents, records, and reports to identify discrepancies or irregularities.
	Perform substantive and analytical audit procedures to assess the accuracy of financial information.


Compliance Assessment:


	Assist in assessing and ensuring compliance with relevant financial regulations, standards, and laws.
	Stay up-to-date with changes in financial regulations and communicate their implications to the team.


Data Analysis:


	Analyze financial data and trends to identify areas for improvement or potential risks.
	Prepare reports summarizing findings and recommendations for clients and internal stakeholders.


Documentation:


	Maintain organized and comprehensive files with all relevant documentation.
	Prepare work papers, audit reports, and other documentation as required.
	Prepare and present reports, summaries, and visualizations for internal and client use.


Client Engagement:


	Collaborate with clients and team members to gather necessary information and address their inquiries.
	Build and maintain strong client relationships by providing excellent service and professional guidance.
	Assist clients in understanding complex financial data and reports.


Risk Assessment:


	Assist in assessing the internal control environment of clients to identify weaknesses and suggest improvements.
	Identify and communicate potential risks to the Partners.


Quality Assurance:


	Ensure that audit procedures are conducted in accordance with professional standards and company policies.
	Participate in quality control reviews and process improvement initiatives.
	Training and Development:
	Continuously update your knowledge and skills related to auditing and financial regulations.
	Actively participate in training programs and mentorship opportunities.


Financial Modelling:


	Build and maintain financial models to support decision-making processes for clients.
	Evaluate the financial performance and viability of various projects.


Confidentiality:


	Maintain the highest level of confidentiality and ethical standards in handling sensitive client information.


Reporting:


	Prepare and present audit findings and recommendations to clients and management.
	Assist in the preparation of audit reports and management letters.


Communication:


	Effectively communicate analysis results and insights to clients and internal stakeholders.
	Collaborate with team members to ensure the delivery of high-quality services.


Continuous Learning:


	Stay current with industry trends, data analysis techniques, and compliance best practices.
	Participate in training and professional development activities.


Job Requirements


	ICAN qualified.
	5 years of experience including audit firm experience; preferably big 4.
	Experience in ERP implementation preferably of Odoo software.


Salary
N400,000 - N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using "Financial Analyst" as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Projects Officer",
  "description": "X-Force Engineering Limited is an engineering and infrastructure solutions company delivering high-quality engineering services across Nigeria and beyond. The company combines technical expertise, innovation, and strong project management to provide reliable engineering solutions.

The company offers services in design, construction, installation, project management, consultancy, and maintenance across civil, structural, mechanical, and electrical engineering disciplines. This integrated approach enables X-Force Engineering to manage projects from concept to completion while maintaining high standards of safety, quality, and efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Chief Projects Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description


	The Chief Projects Officer (CPO) is responsible for leading and overseeing the entire project delivery function of X-Force Engineering Limited.
	The role holder has overall responsibility for five operational units &mdash; HSE, Project Planning, QA/QC, Construction Operations, and Quantity Surveying &mdash; ensuring that all projects are delivered safely, on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
	The Chief Projects Officer provides strategic and operational oversight of the company&#39;s full project portfolio, drives delivery excellence across all sites, and ensures that X-Force Engineering consistently meets and exceeds client expectations on every engagement.


Key Responsibilities and Duties


	Provide strategic leadership and oversight of the Project Management Office, directing the unit heads of HSE, Project Planning, QA/QC, Construction Operations, and Quantity Surveying to ensure cohesive, high-performance project delivery across all active and pipeline projects
	Establish and enforce project delivery standards, methodologies, and governance frameworks across the company&#39;s entire project portfolio, ensuring consistency, accountability, and compliance with contractual, regulatory, and client requirements
	Oversee project planning and scheduling across all projects, ensuring baseline programmes are realistic, resource-loaded, and regularly updated, with schedule and cost variances proactively identified and mitigated; maintain executive oversight of HSE performance across all sites, championing a zero-harm culture and enforcing compliance with Nigerian HSE regulations
	Ensure QA/QC systems and Inspection and Test Plans are effectively implemented on all projects, and oversee the Quantity Surveying function to ensure accurate cost planning, BOQ preparation, variation management, and final account settlements are handled in the company&#39;s commercial interest
	Provide direct executive oversight of the Construction Operations Unit, ensuring all site engineers and construction supervisors are deployed effectively across active project sites; monitor site progress against approved programmes, enforce construction methodology standards, review and approve method statements and inspection test plans, and intervene decisively where site performance, safety, or quality falls below required standards
	Ensure all construction activities across X-Force Engineering project sites comply fully with approved drawings, specifications, and contract requirements; oversee site mobilisation and demobilisation, plant and equipment deployment, subcontractor management, and material procurement in coordination with the relevant unit heads
	Serve as the senior client interface for all project delivery matters &mdash; managing escalations, resolving disputes, chairing progress meetings with key clients, and ensuring client satisfaction is maintained at the highest level across the portfolio
	Report to the Managing Director on overall project portfolio performance, resource utilisation, risk exposure, and financial delivery, and provide delivery capability input to support major business development and tender pursuits


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Portfolio-level project delivery on time &mdash; schedule variance within &plusmn;10% across all active projects
	Portfolio cost performance &mdash; project cost variance within &plusmn;5% of approved budgets company-wide
	Zero lost-time injury (LTI) incidents attributable to HSE management failures across all project sites
	QA/QC compliance &mdash; zero major non-conformances at client or regulatory inspections; NCR close-out rate of 100% within agreed timeframes
	Client satisfaction score of 85% or above across all active projects per quarterly client survey
	Monthly project portfolio performance report submitted to the Managing Director by the 5th of every month


Requirements
Qualifications:


	B.Eng. or B.Sc. in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Mechanical Engineering, or related field from an accredited institution &mdash;&nbsp;Mandatory
	M.Sc. in Project Management, Engineering Management, Construction Management, or MBA &mdash; Strongly Required at this level
	COREN Full Registration (R.Eng.) &mdash; Mandatory
	Membership of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) &mdash;&nbsp;Mandatory


Required Certifications and Licences:


	COREN Full Registration (R.Eng.) &mdash; Mandatory
	NSE Corporate or Fellow Membership (MNSE / FNSE) &mdash;&nbsp;Mandatory
	PMP (Project Management Professional), PRINCE2 Practitioner, or Primavera P6 Professional Certification &mdash;&nbsp;Mandatory (at least one is required; possession of two or more is a strong advantage)
	NEBOSH or equivalent HSE qualification &mdash;&nbsp;Advantage
	FIDIC or NEC Contract Management Certification &mdash;&nbsp;Strong Advantage


Experience:


	Must have 10 to 15 years of post-qualification experience in engineering project management, construction management, or infrastructure delivery, with at least 3 years in a senior leadership or head of department role
	Demonstrated experience managing and directing multi-disciplinary delivery teams encompassing project planners, HSE officers, QA/QC officers, site engineers, and quantity surveyors
	Extensive experience with Nigerian procurement regulations, FIDIC or NEC contract administration, and client management in both public and private sector engineering projects
	Strong background in construction operations and site management &mdash; including method statement review, site programme management, plant and subcontractor oversight, and quality inspection &mdash; with the technical credibility to provide authoritative direction to site engineers and construction supervisors across multiple concurrent project sites
	Track record of implementing project management systems, quality management frameworks, and HSE management plans across multiple simultaneous project sites


Key Competencies and Skills:


	Mastery of project management methodologies (PMI / PRINCE2) and delivery governance across multi-project portfolios
	Proficiency in Primavera P6 and/or MS Project for scheduling, resource loading, and programme management across concurrent projects
	Deep understanding of construction operations &mdash; site sequencing, method statements, plant and labour deployment, subcontractor management, and construction programme control across multiple live project sites
	Exceptional leadership and people management skills &mdash; able to direct, develop, and hold accountable heads of five distinct operational units
	Commercial acumen &mdash; contract administration, cost control, variation management, and claims handling
	Outstanding stakeholder and client management skills, with the confidence to represent the company at senior client and regulatory levels
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with the capacity to make sound decisions under pressure across multiple concurrent project challenges.


Salary
Above N1,000,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@xforceengineeringlimited.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Chief Projects Officer Job at X-Force Engineering Limited




        
    

        
        X-Force Engineering Limited is an engineering and infrastructure solutions company delivering high-quality engineering services across Nigeria and beyond. The company combines technical expertise, innovation, and strong project management to provide reliable engineering solutions.

The company offers services in design, construction, installation, project management, consultancy, and maintenance across civil, structural, mechanical, and electrical engineering disciplines. This integrated approach enables X-Force Engineering to manage projects from concept to completion while maintaining high standards of safety, quality, and efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Projects Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description


	The Chief Projects Officer (CPO) is responsible for leading and overseeing the entire project delivery function of X-Force Engineering Limited.
	The role holder has overall responsibility for five operational units — HSE, Project Planning, QA/QC, Construction Operations, and Quantity Surveying — ensuring that all projects are delivered safely, on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.
	The Chief Projects Officer provides strategic and operational oversight of the company's full project portfolio, drives delivery excellence across all sites, and ensures that X-Force Engineering consistently meets and exceeds client expectations on every engagement.


Key Responsibilities and Duties


	Provide strategic leadership and oversight of the Project Management Office, directing the unit heads of HSE, Project Planning, QA/QC, Construction Operations, and Quantity Surveying to ensure cohesive, high-performance project delivery across all active and pipeline projects
	Establish and enforce project delivery standards, methodologies, and governance frameworks across the company's entire project portfolio, ensuring consistency, accountability, and compliance with contractual, regulatory, and client requirements
	Oversee project planning and scheduling across all projects, ensuring baseline programmes are realistic, resource-loaded, and regularly updated, with schedule and cost variances proactively identified and mitigated; maintain executive oversight of HSE performance across all sites, championing a zero-harm culture and enforcing compliance with Nigerian HSE regulations
	Ensure QA/QC systems and Inspection and Test Plans are effectively implemented on all projects, and oversee the Quantity Surveying function to ensure accurate cost planning, BOQ preparation, variation management, and final account settlements are handled in the company's commercial interest
	Provide direct executive oversight of the Construction Operations Unit, ensuring all site engineers and construction supervisors are deployed effectively across active project sites; monitor site progress against approved programmes, enforce construction methodology standards, review and approve method statements and inspection test plans, and intervene decisively where site performance, safety, or quality falls below required standards
	Ensure all construction activities across X-Force Engineering project sites comply fully with approved drawings, specifications, and contract requirements; oversee site mobilisation and demobilisation, plant and equipment deployment, subcontractor management, and material procurement in coordination with the relevant unit heads
	Serve as the senior client interface for all project delivery matters — managing escalations, resolving disputes, chairing progress meetings with key clients, and ensuring client satisfaction is maintained at the highest level across the portfolio
	Report to the Managing Director on overall project portfolio performance, resource utilisation, risk exposure, and financial delivery, and provide delivery capability input to support major business development and tender pursuits


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Portfolio-level project delivery on time — schedule variance within ±10% across all active projects
	Portfolio cost performance — project cost variance within ±5% of approved budgets company-wide
	Zero lost-time injury (LTI) incidents attributable to HSE management failures across all project sites
	QA/QC compliance — zero major non-conformances at client or regulatory inspections; NCR close-out rate of 100% within agreed timeframes
	Client satisfaction score of 85% or above across all active projects per quarterly client survey
	Monthly project portfolio performance report submitted to the Managing Director by the 5th of every month


Requirements
Qualifications:


	B.Eng. or B.Sc. in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Mechanical Engineering, or related field from an accredited institution — Mandatory
	M.Sc. in Project Management, Engineering Management, Construction Management, or MBA — Strongly Required at this level
	COREN Full Registration (R.Eng.) — Mandatory
	Membership of the Nigerian Society of Engineers (NSE) — Mandatory


Required Certifications and Licences:


	COREN Full Registration (R.Eng.) — Mandatory
	NSE Corporate or Fellow Membership (MNSE / FNSE) — Mandatory
	PMP (Project Management Professional), PRINCE2 Practitioner, or Primavera P6 Professional Certification — Mandatory (at least one is required; possession of two or more is a strong advantage)
	NEBOSH or equivalent HSE qualification — Advantage
	FIDIC or NEC Contract Management Certification — Strong Advantage


Experience:


	Must have 10 to 15 years of post-qualification experience in engineering project management, construction management, or infrastructure delivery, with at least 3 years in a senior leadership or head of department role
	Demonstrated experience managing and directing multi-disciplinary delivery teams encompassing project planners, HSE officers, QA/QC officers, site engineers, and quantity surveyors
	Extensive experience with Nigerian procurement regulations, FIDIC or NEC contract administration, and client management in both public and private sector engineering projects
	Strong background in construction operations and site management — including method statement review, site programme management, plant and subcontractor oversight, and quality inspection — with the technical credibility to provide authoritative direction to site engineers and construction supervisors across multiple concurrent project sites
	Track record of implementing project management systems, quality management frameworks, and HSE management plans across multiple simultaneous project sites


Key Competencies and Skills:


	Mastery of project management methodologies (PMI / PRINCE2) and delivery governance across multi-project portfolios
	Proficiency in Primavera P6 and/or MS Project for scheduling, resource loading, and programme management across concurrent projects
	Deep understanding of construction operations — site sequencing, method statements, plant and labour deployment, subcontractor management, and construction programme control across multiple live project sites
	Exceptional leadership and people management skills — able to direct, develop, and hold accountable heads of five distinct operational units
	Commercial acumen — contract administration, cost control, variation management, and claims handling
	Outstanding stakeholder and client management skills, with the confidence to represent the company at senior client and regulatory levels
	Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with the capacity to make sound decisions under pressure across multiple concurrent project challenges.


Salary
Above N1,000,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
